changes_summary: Added Step 2 (stale-id validation guard) directly addressing FLAG-001: once timelines.data arrives, if the URL's ?timeline= isn't in the live list, the new effect clears lastTimelineId and the param so the existing fallback/auto-create branch runs. Confirmed via SupabaseDataProvider.ts:51-71 that loadTimeline silently returns createDefaultTimelineConfig() for missing rows, so the flag's concern was real. Also tightened the persist effect at 202–208 to skip writing stale ids back. Renumbered remaining steps; expanded the Overview to call out the provider behavior and the recovery strategy; added a manual smoke scenario for the deleted-id case; promoted the recovery behavior to a must-priority success criterion.
questions: Is the recovery strategy (clear param + lastTimelineId, then let the auto-select effect re-seed from timelines.data[0]) acceptable, or do you prefer a more visible UX (e.g., a one-time toast saying 'Last timeline was removed')?
For the Opt+Shift+W global shortcut, when the React Query cache is cold should we navigate without the param (and rely on Step 1 page-level seeding), or perform an async prefetch first?

success_criteria.criterion: VideoEditorPage no longer renders the 'Opening video editor / Loading your latest timeline…' Card; that branch returns null instead.
The auto-select effect seeds ?timeline=<id> from settings.lastTimelineId before consulting timelines.data, and no longer waits on timelines.isLoading to do so.

When the cached lastTimelineId points at a timeline not present in timelines.data, the page clears ?timeline= and lastTimelineId, then re-seeds via timelines.data[0] or auto-create — the user is never stranded in an empty editor.
The persist effect (currently 202–208) does not write a stale (non-existent) timelineId back to settings.
The timelines.error branch in VideoEditorPage still renders its 'Unable to open video editor' Card unchanged.
Navigation from the ToolsPane tool card to /tools/video-editor appends ?timeline=<lastTimelineId> when a cached value is available.
Opt+Shift+W global shortcut appends ?timeline=<lastTimelineId> when the value is in the React Query cache.
VideoEditorPage still auto-creates a default timeline on first project visit.
Last persisted timeline id continues to be written back to settings when the user opens a valid timeline (existing 202–208 behavior, modulo the stale-skip guard).
pnpm tsc --noEmit passes with no new type errors.
No dangling Skeleton/Card imports left in VideoEditorPage after removing the loading card.
Manual smoke: returning project with valid cached id shows the editor shell directly with no placeholder flash.
Manual smoke: returning project with a deleted cached id recovers without showing an empty editor.
The refactor to hoist editor chrome out of VideoEditorProvider is NOT performed.

assumptions: The React Query cache key used by useToolSettings is stable and can be peeked from useGlobalPaneShortcuts via queryClient.getQueryData without triggering a fetch.
ToolsPane is always mounted once the user is in a project, so subscribing to useToolSettings there is effectively a session-long prefetch with negligible cost.

Clearing ?timeline= via setSearchParams({}, { replace: true }) is a sufficient signal for the auto-select effect to re-run and pick timelines.data[0] / auto-create. (VideoEditorPage already keys re-runs off timelineId.)
Writing `update('project', { lastTimelineId: undefined })` is a supported way to clear the persisted setting (the existing delete handler at line 158 already does this).
videoEditorSettings.id is importable in ToolsPane without creating a circular dependency (toolManifest already imports it).
No existing unit test asserts the presence of the 'Opening video editor' Card; if one exists it will be updated to assert the null state.

The stale-id guard's small additional check inside the persist effect (early-return when timelines.data is loaded and the id is not in the list) is preferable to reordering effect declarations, since React effect ordering is fragile.
